title: Week 1 Reflection: Why Access Isn't Enough
reflection: After spending three days in Pine Bluff, one thing is clear: having internet access and having meaningful STEM engagement are two very different things. Students here can technically access online courses, but without mentors, role models, or a culture that sees STEM as a viable path, the access feels hollow. One student told me, 'I can Google anything, but that doesn't mean I know what to Google.' That stuck with me.

title: Week 2 Reflection: The One-Teacher Problem
reflection: Mrs. Patterson teaches biology, chemistry, physics, and earth science — all by herself. She's brilliant and dedicated, but she's one person covering what most suburban schools split across four or five teachers. The depth students can reach in any one subject is limited by time, not talent. How do we support teachers like her without burning them out?
